controls.title: Configuration standards are developed, implemented, and maintained for system components
Strong cryptography protects PAN during transmission over open, public networks (valid certificates, trusted keys)
Software engineering techniques prevent or mitigate common software attacks (injection, data-structure, cryptography-usage, and access-control attacks)
System components are protected from known vulnerabilities via applicable security patches and updates
Idle user sessions are locked or terminated after inactivity
Strong cryptography renders authentication factors unreadable during storage and transmission
Invalid authentication attempts are limited by account lockout
Passwords/passphrases meet minimum complexity and length
Passwords/passphrases for application and system accounts are not hard-coded in scripts, configuration files, or source code
Audit logs are enabled and active for all system components and cardholder data

notAssessable.title: Install and Maintain Network Security Controls
notAssessable.reason: Requires live network architecture and rule-set review, not source scanning.

notAssessable.title: Protect Stored Account Data
notAssessable.reason: Requires data discovery and data-flow evidence; a scanner cannot see where account data actually lives.

notAssessable.title: Protect All Systems and Networks from Malicious Software
notAssessable.reason: Anti-malware deployment is an operational control on live systems.

notAssessable.title: Restrict Access to System Components by Business Need to Know
notAssessable.reason: Access authorization is an organizational process, not visible in code.

notAssessable.title: Restrict Physical Access to Cardholder Data
notAssessable.reason: Physical controls cannot be assessed by any scanner.

notAssessable.title: Test Security of Systems and Networks Regularly
notAssessable.reason: A testing program is organizational; this platform's scans can serve as evidence inside such a program, but cannot attest to it.

notAssessable.title: Support Information Security with Organizational Policies and Programs
notAssessable.reason: Policies and governance are documents and processes, not code.
